technical_explanation: A numerical reputation tracker for NPC groups. Affects shop prices, quest availability, and whether guards are hostile or helpful.
A navigation mode where the map is hidden by Fog of War and divided into tiles. Requires logic for 'getting lost' and procedural resource discovery.
A status-effect tracker that applies permanent physical or mental debuffs/buffs to the player character based on exposure to eldritch energy.
A secondary combat resource (Psi Points) that bypasses standard 'Magic Resistance' and often requires Intelligence or Wisdom saving throws.
A multi-variable 'Approval' meter. Player choices in dialogue add or subtract points from different factions; high scores unlock 'Army' assets for the endgame.
A hidden or visible meter that depletes upon seeing horrors. Low levels cause 'Short-term Madness' (stuns/confusion) or 'Long-term Madness' (permanent traits).
Survival logic that forces 'Hunger' and 'Thirst' debuffs. Players must spend actions or gold on 'Rations' and 'Water' to prevent Exhaustion levels.
A randomization script run at the start of a new game. It determines the coordinates of key quest items and the identity of a 'Fated Ally' NPC.
An environmental 'kill-zone' or 'teleport-back' loop that prevents the player from leaving the designated map area until a specific flag is set.
A crafting and gear-socketing system where players find 'Runes' to enchant weapons or armor with unique passive abilities.
A global modifier that disables all 'Revivify' or 'Resurrection' spells. Players who reach 0 HP and fail saves are permanently deleted/unplayable.
A world-state toggle (Spring, Summer, Autumn, Winter) that changes NPC schedules, lighting presets, and which 'Main Boss' script is active.
A vehicular combat physics engine. Includes logic for fuel (Soul Coins), mounting weapons, and high-speed collision damage.
A secondary currency used exclusively for high-tier merchants and fueling vehicles. Often involves 'Moral Alignment' hits when spent.
A script that tracks a secondary group of NPCs moving across the map at the same speed as the player. They can 'claim' quest items before the player does.
A global gravity and movement modifier. Disadvantages melee attacks (unless using specific weapons) and introduces 3D vertical movement (Swimming).
A time-based buff/debuff system linked to the world's moons. Certain subclasses gain power based on the current 'Moon Phase' in the game clock.
A level-loading system that swaps out the entire 'Skybox', 'Gravity', and 'Physics' constants as players move between dimensions.
